failure_summary: The parent reward produces consistent partial_progress trajectories but no success_like outcomes; terminal bonuses (safe_landing_bonus, intermediate_safe_bonus) have near-zero alignment and do not fire effectively. Gated_timeout_penalty penalizes partial_progress more than failures, impeding conversion to success.

main_useful_patterns: partial_progress trajectories show strong approach_progress and near_stage_stability discrimination; these components reliably separate partial_progress from failures
uncertainties: No high-confidence success_like trajectories exist, so success-level preferences cannot be constructed
Partial progress should not be treated as task success
Terminal bonus components have near-zero firing rates, making alignment uncertain

component_diagnosis.evidence: Component summary shows strong discrimination: failure_mean 1.80, positive_mean 4.61. Repeated archival lessons emphasize preserving this component.
Component summary shows very strong discrimination: failure_mean 0.007, positive_mean 36.1, indicating it is key to partial_progress generation.
Fires occasionally but near-zero mean; minor incentive for leg contact when near pad. Not harmful, preserve.
Component summary shows all zeros; code conditions are too strict. Needs progressive thresholds to actually fire for promising states.
Component summary shows positive_mean 0.052, effectively not firing. Current discrete thresholds (relaxed/strict) are not reached. Must be redesigned with progressive, continuous shaping.
Component summary all zeros. Code conditions likely never triggered; may be removed or restructured but not detrimental.

Component summary shows positive_mean -0.532 (penalty) applied more strongly to partial_progress than failures (failure_mean -0.175). This penalizes desired behavior. Must be gated to only apply to trajectories with negligible progress (delta_dist <= 0 and curr_dist > 0.5) as per archival lessons.

forbidden_changes: do not treat partial_progress as success_like
do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not only scale all coefficients
do not remove approach_progress or near_stage_stability
required_changes: restructure terminal bonuses (safe_landing_bonus, intermediate_safe_bonus) with progressive, continuous thresholds that become more stringent as the lander approaches the pad
restructure gated_timeout_penalty to only penalize trajectories with negligible progress (delta_dist <= 0 and curr_dist > 0.5)
ensure new terminal bonus components have nonzero firing rates and positive alignment with partial_progress > failure preferences
mutation_intent.remove_or_gate_components_notes: gated_timeout_penalty should be gated to negligible progress only; gated_failure_penalty can be removed if ineffective

rollback_decision.reason: Hard rollback is handled by RollbackManager; current memory contains weak but usable partial-progress evidence and the parent reward preserved partial_progress count at 50. Previous round6 candidate1_progress_conditioned has similar partial_progress preservation, but no substantial regression warrants rolling back.

self_evaluation_lesson: After 9 rounds, the cumulative lesson is clear: approach_progress and near_stage_stability are essential for partial_progress generation, but terminal bonuses need structural—not just coefficient—evolution to bridge to success_like. Progressive shaping of safe landing conditions is necessary, and any penalty that applies to partial_progress trajectories must be carefully gated. TAGE scores on memory do not predict training return or success_like conversion, so we must prioritize structural changes over parameter tuning.
